notes_md:
**vươn** = to reach or stretch out/up toward something. It’s used for physical stretching and also for growth or progress.

Often used with body parts (tay, cổ, người) when you physically extend yourself. It also describes plants or objects extending outward.

It can mean “strive” or “rise up” in a more abstract way (personal growth, success).


Close memory link: similar to *với* (reach for), but *vươn* feels more like stretching your whole body or making an effort upward.
exampleSentences: Mỗi sáng, anh thường vươn tay lên trời để tập thể dục.
Khi muốn lấy quyển sách trên kệ cao, em phải vươn người lên.
Cây ngoài vườn đang vươn những cành non về phía ánh sáng.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Every morning, I usually reach out my hands to the sky to exercise.
When I want to get the book on the high shelf, I have to reach out.
The tree in the garden is reach out its young branches toward the light.

definitions.vi: Vươn ra hoặc với tới một cái gì đó.
definitions.en: To stretch out or reach for something.

example.vi: Cây vươn cao về phía ánh sáng.
example.en: The tree reaches up towards the light.
example.es: El árbol se extiende hacia la luz.
